DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO A FEW FARMSTEADS 8-9 MILES SOUTH AND ROUGHLY 1 MILE WEST OF SUTTON. TIME WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND FROM AN AREA COOP OBSERVER. CARPORTS AT 2 LOCATIONS WERE DESTROYED, ONE WAS BLOWN OUT INTO A NEIGHBORING FIELD. TWO HOG SHEDS WERE BADLY DAMAGED AT ANOTHER LOCATION. ONE HOME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. THE ROOF OF THE GARAGE WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED (AT LEAST PARTIALLY REMOVED). THE ROOF OF THE HOUSE WAS DAMAGED WHERE IT MEETS THE GARAGE, ALLOWING THE HEAVY RAIN FROM THE REST OF THE EVENT TO ENTER INTO THE HOME. OBSERVER NOTED THINGS WERE ALL BLOWN IN THE SAME DIRECTION.
REPORT OF DAMAGE 8 TO 9 MILES SOUTH OF SUTTON AND 1 WEST. DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A DETACHED GARAGE AND PART OF THE ROOF WAS GONE TO THE HOUSE. WATER IN THE BASEMENT AND TWO SMALL HOG SHEDS OVERTURNED.

A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NW YORK COUNTY ON MONDAY AUGUST 6 AT 743 PM CDT. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH OF ABOUT 1.6 MILES AND WAS ROUGHLY 125 YDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT. PEAK WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. TIME WAS DETERMINED FROM REPORTS AND RADAR DATA. THE TORNADO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOUSE 7 MILES N OF BRADSHAW, MAINLY TO SOFFITS, GARAGE DOORS AND SOME ROOF DAMAGE. GRAIN BINS WERE DAMAGED ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREES. SOME LARGER CEDAR TREES WERE SNAPPED 2 TO 3 FT ABOVE GROUND. IRRIGATION PIVOTS WERE ALSO DAMAGED.

ADDITIONAL DETAILS FOR PREVIOUSLY-ISSUED LSR. DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE AT ROAD 21 AND ROAD E. TREES AND POLES BLOWN OVER AND GRAIN BINS DESTROYED. DAMAGE TO HOME INCLUDED GARAGE DOORS BULGED OUTWARD AND CONSIDERABLE SOFFIT DAMAGE.

COMBINATION OF HAIL/WIND RESULTED IN EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE IN THE AREA EXTENDING FROM TILDEN ROAD /1 W FARWELL/ EAST TO THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 11 AND 92...THEN SOUTH TOWARD 8TH AVENUE /NEAR DANNEBROG/. IN THE FARWELL AREA...TREE/WINDOW/ROOF/SIDING DAMAGE WAS REPORTED. RURAL POWER DISTRICT WAS WORKING TO RESTORE POWER...AN ESTIMATED 12 POWER POLES WERE DAMAGE/SNAPPED IN THE FARWELL/DANNEBROG AREA. PIVOTS WERE KNOCKED OVER...SOME GRAIN BIN DAMAGE...ONE GRAIN BIN ESE OF FARWELL WAS BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATION. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
